en: I've been studying it my whole life.
It's just that it's so theoretical, you know?
I mean, you know, maybe there's a more concrete approach.
Here, let's try this. Oh, God!
Michael, what did you do? I made the trolley problem real
so we could see how the ethics would actually play out.
ko: 우리가 윤리가 실제로 어떻게 작용하는지 볼 수 있도록.

en: There are five workers on this track and one over there.
ko: 이 선로에는 다섯 명의 작업자가 있고 저쪽에는 한 명이 있어.

en: Here are the levers to switch the tracks. Make a choice.
ko: 여기 선로를 전환할 레버가 있어. 선택해.

en: The thing is, I mean, ethically speaking... No time, dude! Make a decision!
ko: 문제는, 윤리적으로 말하자면... 시간 없어, 친구!

en: Well, it's tricky! I mean, on the one hand, if you ascribe to a purely utilitarian world, you...
Okay, so... What did we learn? Chidi?
Talk it out, buddy. What are we thinking?
He thinks he just killed a bunch of people with a trolley. It's just a simulation.
ko: 그는 트롤리로 많은 사람을 죽였다고 생각해.

en: I would never make you kill real people. Oh, well, that's reassuring,
ko: 나는 절대 너에게 실제 사람을 죽이라고 하지 않을 거야.

en: because some of the parts of the fake people
